pattern: Each maximal run of length L contributes L*(L+1)/2 homogenous substrings; sum over runs modulo 1e9+7.
hints: A homogenous substring lives entirely inside one maximal run of identical characters.
A run of length L contains exactly L*(L+1)/2 contiguous substrings, all homogenous.
Scan the string, measure each run length, add its triangular number, and take the result modulo 1000000007.

edge_inputs.description: alternating, no run longer than one
long uniform run to exercise the modulus arithmetic

stress.description: long string over a tiny alphabet to produce long runs

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def countHomogenous(self, s):
        MOD = 10 ** 9 + 7
        total = 0
        i = 0
        n = len(s)
        while i < n:
            j = i
            while j < n and s[j] == s[i]:
                j += 1
            L = j - i
            total += L * (L + 1) // 2
            i = j
        return total % MOD
